highlight.title: Cloud Run's Billing Caps and AI Integration
highlight.summary: <cite index="2-14,2-15,10-29,10-30">Cloud Run introduces billing caps to prevent cost overruns by deactivating resources when spending limits are reached. Coupled with GPU support for RTX 6000 Pro and Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform integration, this makes serverless AI workloads more accessible.</cite>

resources.title: Cloud Run Worker Pools and CREMA for AI Workloads
resources.why_now: <cite index="3-27,3-28,3-29,3-30">Worker pools are now GA for pull-based workloads like AI inference, with the open-sourced CREMA autoscaler enabling queue-aware scaling based on Pub/Sub backlog or Kafka lag.</cite>

resources.title: Secret Manager with Pub/Sub Integration Patterns
resources.why_now: <cite index="21-2,21-3,21-13,21-14">Secret Manager's notification-based rotation via Pub/Sub requires Cloud Functions or Cloud Run services to handle the actual credential updates, making automation patterns critical for production systems.</cite>

resources.title: App Engine Cost Optimization Through Scaling Controls
resources.why_now: <cite index="14-5,14-6,14-7,14-8">Auto-scaling settings aligned with traffic demands can reduce costs by up to 30%, with sustained-use discounts providing additional 30% reduction for long-running workloads.</cite>

resources.title: Event-Driven Microservices Architecture Patterns for 2026
resources.why_now: <cite index="33-15,33-16,33-19">Event-driven architectures using Apache Kafka and Pulsar are becoming standard for microservices, providing better decoupling and resilience as systems scale.</cite>

claude_take: The big shift is serverless becoming production-ready with proper cost controls and AI integration, while traditional App Engine remains cost-effective for steady workloads when properly configured. Secret management automation through Pub/Sub patterns is becoming essential as teams move beyond manual processes.
